name: typescript-language-server
description: Language server for TypeScript and JavaScript providing code completion, go-to-definition, hover info, refactoring, and real-time diagnostics via the Language Server Protocol
when_to_use: Always active for JS/TS projects — provides IDE intelligence including type inference, auto-imports, and error detection

name: tsc
description: TypeScript compiler — transpiles TypeScript to JavaScript, generates declaration files, and performs full type checking
when_to_use: To compile TypeScript projects, generate .d.ts declaration files, or run a full type-checked build

name: vite build
description: Vite bundler — fast frontend build tool using native ES modules in dev and Rollup for optimized production bundles
when_to_use: To bundle a Vite-based project for production or start the dev server with instant HMR

name: esbuild
description: Extremely fast JavaScript and TypeScript bundler and minifier written in Go — 10-100x faster than traditional bundlers
when_to_use: When build speed is critical, for bundling libraries, or as a fast transpiler in larger build pipelines

name: webpack
description: Module bundler for JavaScript applications — handles code splitting, tree shaking, asset management, and plugin-based transformations
when_to_use: For complex build pipelines requiring fine-grained control over bundling, code splitting, and asset processing

name: npm
description: Node Package Manager — installs, updates, and manages JavaScript dependencies from the npm registry
when_to_use: To install project dependencies, add new packages, update versions, or manage the dependency tree

name: pnpm
description: Fast, disk space efficient package manager — uses content-addressable storage to deduplicate dependencies across projects
when_to_use: As a drop-in npm replacement with faster installs, strict dependency resolution, and excellent monorepo support via workspaces

name: bun
description: All-in-one JavaScript runtime, package manager, bundler, and test runner — significantly faster than Node.js for many operations
when_to_use: As a fast alternative to Node.js + npm for running scripts, installing packages, bundling, and running tests

name: jest
description: Full-featured JavaScript testing framework with built-in assertion library, mocking, code coverage, and snapshot testing
when_to_use: To run unit and integration tests, generate code coverage reports, or use snapshot testing for UI components
what_it_finds: Test failures, assertion errors, snapshot mismatches, uncovered code paths

name: vitest
description: Vite-native testing framework — blazing fast with native ESM support, Jest-compatible API, and built-in TypeScript support without configuration
when_to_use: For testing Vite-based projects or any JS/TS project wanting fast tests with zero-config TypeScript and ESM support
what_it_finds: Test failures, assertion errors, type errors during testing, coverage gaps

name: playwright
description: End-to-end browser testing framework by Microsoft — tests across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit with auto-waiting, tracing, and codegen
when_to_use: For E2E browser testing, cross-browser compatibility testing, visual regression testing, and API testing
what_it_finds: UI regressions, broken user flows, cross-browser issues, accessibility violations, network errors

name: cypress
description: End-to-end testing framework with interactive test runner, time-travel debugging, automatic waiting, and real-time reloading
when_to_use: For E2E and component testing with a visual interactive runner, especially in CI pipelines with Cypress Cloud
what_it_finds: Broken user flows, UI regressions, flaky tests, network request issues, DOM state problems

name: eslint
description: Pluggable JavaScript and TypeScript linter — identifies and auto-fixes code quality issues, enforces coding standards, and catches potential bugs
when_to_use: Before commits and in CI to enforce code quality, catch bugs early, and maintain consistent coding standards
what_it_finds: Unused variables, unreachable code, missing error handling, style violations, potential bugs, accessibility issues

name: prettier
description: Opinionated code formatter — enforces consistent style across JavaScript, TypeScript, JSON, CSS, HTML, Markdown, and more
when_to_use: Before every commit to ensure consistent code formatting, or integrated into editor save hooks

name: biome
description: Fast linter and formatter written in Rust — replaces ESLint and Prettier with a single tool that is 20-100x faster
when_to_use: As a unified, high-performance alternative to ESLint + Prettier for linting and formatting JS/TS projects
what_it_finds: Code style violations, potential bugs, unused imports, complexity issues — same categories as ESLint but faster

name: tsc --noEmit
description: TypeScript type checker without emitting output — validates types across the entire project quickly without producing compiled files
when_to_use: As a fast type-checking step in CI or before commits to catch type errors without running a full build
what_it_finds: Type mismatches, missing properties, incorrect function signatures, null/undefined errors, generic constraint violations

name: npm audit
description: Scans the dependency tree for known security vulnerabilities using the npm advisory database
when_to_use: Before releases, in CI pipelines, after dependency updates, or as a regular security check
what_it_finds: CVEs in direct and transitive dependencies, severity ratings, remediation paths, vulnerable package versions

name: depcheck
description: Analyzes dependencies in a project to find unused dependencies, missing dependencies, and phantom dependencies
when_to_use: To clean up package.json, reduce bundle size, and ensure all imports have corresponding declared dependencies
what_it_finds: Unused dependencies listed in package.json, dependencies used in code but not declared, devDependency misclassifications

name: bundlesize
description: Monitors JavaScript bundle sizes against configurable thresholds — fails CI if bundles exceed size budgets
when_to_use: In CI pipelines to enforce bundle size budgets, prevent size regressions, and track bundle growth over time
what_it_finds: Bundle size violations, files exceeding size limits, size regression between builds

name: husky
description: Git hooks manager — automatically runs scripts on git events like pre-commit, pre-push, and commit-msg
when_to_use: To enforce linting, formatting, and tests before commits and pushes via git hooks

install.command: npm install -D husky && npx husky init

name: lint-staged
description: Runs linters and formatters against only staged git files — makes pre-commit hooks fast by avoiding full-project scans
when_to_use: In pre-commit hooks (with husky) to lint and format only changed files, keeping commits fast

name: changesets
description: Version management and changelog generation tool for monorepos — tracks changes per package and automates releases
when_to_use: To manage versioning in monorepos, generate changelogs from structured change descriptions, and automate npm publishing

name: turbo
description: High-performance monorepo build system by Vercel — incremental builds with remote caching, task parallelization, and dependency-aware scheduling
when_to_use: For monorepo build orchestration with intelligent caching, parallel task execution, and CI optimization

name: nx
description: Monorepo toolkit with computation caching, task orchestration, code generation, dependency graph visualization, and affected-only command execution
when_to_use: For large monorepos needing project scaffolding, dependency graph analysis, affected-only CI runs, and distributed task execution

name: ESLint
description: The de-facto linter for JavaScript and TypeScript — pluggable rule system, autofix, huge ecosystem of configs and plugins
when_to_use: Any JS/TS project that wants automated code-quality enforcement in editor and CI

name: Prettier
description: Opinionated formatter for JavaScript, TypeScript, JSON, CSS, Markdown, YAML — enforces consistent style with minimal config
when_to_use: Any project that wants a single source of truth for formatting across many file types

name: Biome
description: Rust-based toolchain for JS/TS — combines lint, format, and import sort in one fast binary as an ESLint+Prettier alternative
when_to_use: When you want a single unified tool that replaces ESLint + Prettier with much faster execution

name: TypeScript compiler
description: The official TypeScript compiler — type-checks and transpiles TypeScript source to JavaScript
when_to_use: Every TypeScript project; use `tsc --noEmit` in CI for type-only checks

name: Jest
description: Delightful JavaScript testing framework — zero-config setup, snapshot testing, built-in mocking
when_to_use: Default testing framework for React, Node.js, and many JS ecosystem projects

name: Vitest
description: Vite-native unit test framework with Jest-compatible API, ESM-first, lightning-fast hot reload
when_to_use: For Vite-based projects or when you want faster tests than Jest with the same assertion API

name: Playwright
description: End-to-end testing framework for modern web apps — drives Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit with a single API
when_to_use: E2E tests for web apps; cross-browser validation; automated screenshots and video traces

name: Vite
description: Next-generation frontend build tool — native ESM dev server with instant HMR and an optimized Rollup-based production build
when_to_use: Modern frontend projects (React, Vue, Svelte) that want fast dev startup and modern build output

name: esbuild
description: Extremely fast JavaScript bundler and minifier written in Go — often 10-100x faster than traditional tools
when_to_use: When build speed is critical; as a bundler inside other tools or for library builds

name: pnpm
description: Fast, disk-space-efficient JavaScript package manager using a content-addressable store to avoid duplicating dependencies
when_to_use: When npm/yarn are too slow or wasteful; excellent for monorepos and CI caching

name: bun
description: All-in-one JavaScript runtime, package manager, test runner, and bundler built for speed — Node.js-compatible with JSC under the hood
when_to_use: When you want a faster alternative to Node+npm+jest with minimal switching cost

name: Deno
description: Secure JS/TS runtime built on V8, Rust, and Tokio — native TS, permission-based security, URL-based imports
when_to_use: Scripts or services that want TypeScript without a build step and strong security defaults

name: Snyk
description: Developer-first security platform — scans dependencies, code, containers, and IaC for vulnerabilities
when_to_use: In CI to block builds with known-vulnerable dependencies; free tier for OSS

name: TypeDoc
description: Documentation generator for TypeScript — reads TS source with JSDoc-style comments and produces HTML or Markdown
when_to_use: TypeScript libraries that want API reference documentation automatically generated from the source

name: Mocha
description: Flexible JavaScript test framework with support for BDD/TDD interfaces, any assertion library, and extensive reporter ecosystem
when_to_use: When you need a flexible test runner that works with any assertion library (Chai, Should.js) and supports async patterns

name: Storybook
description: UI component workshop for building, testing, and documenting components in isolation — supports React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, and Web Components
when_to_use: For developing UI components in isolation, visual testing, generating living component documentation, and design system development

name: Rollup
description: ES module bundler optimized for library builds — produces clean, tree-shakeable output in ESM, CJS, and UMD formats
when_to_use: For bundling JavaScript libraries where output quality and tree-shaking matter more than application bundling features

name: SWC
description: Super-fast Rust-based JavaScript/TypeScript compiler — drop-in replacement for Babel with 20-70x better performance
when_to_use: When Babel is too slow, as a fast transpiler in build pipelines, or as the compiler backend for Next.js and Vite

name: Socket
description: Supply chain security tool that detects malicious and risky npm packages before installation — analyzes package behavior, not just known CVEs
when_to_use: Before installing new dependencies, in CI to catch supply chain attacks, typosquatting, and protestware

name: Standard
description: Zero-config JavaScript linter and formatter — enforces a single consistent style with no .eslintrc or prettier config needed
when_to_use: JavaScript projects that want opinionated linting with zero configuration — no semicolons, 2-space indent

name: XO
description: Opinionated ESLint wrapper with sensible defaults — TypeScript support out of the box, Prettier-compatible, zero-config for most projects
when_to_use: When you want ESLint with strong defaults, automatic TypeScript and Prettier integration, and minimal config

name: JSDoc
description: API documentation generator for JavaScript — parses JSDoc comment annotations and produces HTML reference documentation
when_to_use: JavaScript projects (non-TypeScript) that want generated API docs from source code comments

name: tsup
description: Zero-config TypeScript bundler powered by esbuild — produces ESM, CJS, and IIFE outputs with .d.ts generation
when_to_use: For quickly bundling TypeScript libraries without configuring Rollup or webpack — ideal for npm packages

name: tsx
description: Node.js enhanced with esbuild to run TypeScript and ESM files directly — zero-config, fast startup, supports watch mode
when_to_use: Running TypeScript scripts and servers without a build step — replaces ts-node with faster execution

name: size-limit
description: Performance budget tool for JavaScript — calculates the real cost of your library including parse time, not just gzip size
when_to_use: In CI to enforce JavaScript size budgets with accurate cost modeling including loading and parse time

name: Knip
description: Finds unused files, dependencies, and exports in TypeScript and JavaScript projects — comprehensive dead code detection
when_to_use: To identify and remove dead code, unused dependencies, unreferenced exports, and unused config entries
